channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Plan mode is now available in Gemini CLI
item.description: Gemini CLI has introduced Plan Mode, a read-only feature that allows developers to analyze codebases and plan architectural changes without executing code. This mode enhances collaboration and reduces the risk of errors during development.

item.title: Announcing the Colab MCP Server: Connect Any AI Agent to Google Colab
item.description: The Colab MCP Server has been launched, enabling AI agents like Gemini CLI and Claude Code to connect seamlessly with Google Colab for local prototyping. This integration simplifies the development process for AI applications.

item.title: Developer’s Guide to AI Agent Protocols
item.description: A new guide introduces six AI agent protocols, including MCP and A2A, aimed at standardizing data access and communication for developers. The guide uses a kitchen manager agent example to illustrate practical applications.

item.title: Build a smart financial assistant with LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1
item.description: A tutorial demonstrates how to build a financial assistant using LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1, focusing on extracting data from unstructured documents. The workflow aims to improve the processing of complex financial information.

item.title: Jump to play: Building with Gemini & MediaPipe
item.description: This article outlines a workflow for developing motion-controlled games using Gemini and MediaPipe, emphasizing rapid prototyping and optimization for responsive gameplay. It highlights the importance of refining game mechanics effectively.

item.title: Closing the knowledge gap with agent skills
item.description: Google DeepMind has developed a 'Gemini API developer skill' to enhance AI agents' performance by providing live documentation and SDK guidance, significantly improving success rates in tasks. This approach bridges knowledge gaps in evolving software practices.

item.title: Announcing ADK for Java 1.0.0: Building the Future of AI Agents in Java
item.description: Google has released version 1.0.0 of the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Java, featuring new capabilities for agent control and collaboration across frameworks. The update aims to enhance the development of AI agents in Java.

item.title: Copilot data residency in US + EU and FedRAMP compliance now available
item.description: GitHub Copilot now offers data residency options for US and EU regions, ensuring compliance with local data regulations. This feature is particularly beneficial for US government customers requiring data security.

item.title: The new rules for AI-assisted code in the Linux kernel: What every dev needs to know
item.description: The Linux kernel maintainers have established new rules for AI-assisted coding, though some experts believe these rules may not fully address the challenges posed by AI-generated code. This highlights ongoing concerns in the developer community.

item.title: Why opinion on AI is so divided
item.description: The Stanford AI Index provides an annual overview of AI trends and opinions, revealing a divided perspective on AI's impact and future. This report serves as a reflective tool for industry stakeholders.

item.title: Notepad sheds Copilot from toolbar as Microsoft gives subtlety a try
item.description: Microsoft has removed the Copilot feature from the Notepad toolbar, opting for a more subtle integration under 'Writing Tools'. This change reflects a shift towards a simpler user experience in basic text editing.

item.title: Booking.com warns reservation data may have checked out with intruders
item.description: Booking.com has alerted customers that their reservation data, including personal details, may have been compromised by hackers. This incident highlights ongoing vulnerabilities in the travel industry’s data security.

item.title: Microsoft attempts to untangle 'confusing' Windows Insider program
item.description: Microsoft is revamping its Windows Insider program to eliminate confusion surrounding its Controlled Feature Rollouts. The changes aim to streamline user participation and feedback.

item.title: How I squeeze more power from my portable solar panels: 11 ways to get up to 30% extra
item.description: A guide on maximizing the efficiency of portable solar panels reveals that small adjustments can lead to significant power gains. The article shares 11 practical tips based on extensive testing.

item.title: Veterans Affairs has lost track of software licenses amid $985M bill
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is struggling to manage its software licenses, leading to a $985 million bill. A federal watchdog has identified the need for better systems to track licensing.

item.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
item.description: The 2026 AI Index report from Stanford University provides insights into the current state of AI, addressing misconceptions and presenting data on its impact. It aims to clarify the ongoing AI debate.

item.title: I see why Ubuntu 26.04 is more than just a performance bump for thrill-seeking gamers
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 impresses with its performance enhancements and new applications, catering to gamers. The release also indicates a shift in focus towards user experience.

item.title: UK defense startup to supply drone interceptors for Britain and allies
item.description: The UK Ministry of Defence plans to procure drone interceptors from Cambridge Aerospace to enhance defense capabilities against Iranian drones. This move aims to bolster security for Britain and its allies.

item.title: France is replacing 2.5 million Windows desktops with Linux - and I mapped out its new stack
item.description: France is set to replace 2.5 million Windows desktops with Linux, though details about the new system remain unclear. This transition reflects a significant shift in the country's IT strategy.

item.title: The Download: how humans make decisions, and Moderna’s “vaccine” word games
item.description: Today's edition of The Download newsletter discusses human decision-making and Moderna's communication strategies regarding vaccines. It provides a snapshot of current tech-related topics.

item.title: I bought an earwax camera for my toolkit and use it for everything but my ears
item.description: The Bebird Earsight Plus D39R earwax removal tool has proven to be a versatile inspection camera, useful for various tasks beyond ear cleaning. Its performance makes it a valuable addition to any toolkit.

item.title: Adobe finally patches PDF pest after months of abuse
item.description: Adobe has patched a critical zero-day vulnerability in Acrobat and Reader that allowed attackers to exploit booby-trapped PDF documents for months. This fix is crucial for user security against targeted attacks.

item.title: Gym giant Basic-Fit confirms data on a million members stolen in cyberattack
item.description: Basic-Fit, Europe's largest gym chain, has confirmed that a cyberattack resulted in the theft of personal data from around a million members, including sensitive bank details. This incident raises concerns about data security in the fitness industry.

item.title: As an Android user, this MagSafe wallet is the clearest reason why Qi2 magnets shouldn't be ignored
item.description: The Veger X5 combines a MagSafe charger, wallet, and device tracker, showcasing the potential of Qi2 magnets for Android users. Its multifunctionality makes it a compelling accessory for tech-savvy consumers.

item.title: The best Zoom alternatives in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: With increasing security concerns and connectivity issues, this article reviews the best alternatives to Zoom for remote work video conferencing. It aims to guide users towards more reliable options.

item.title: Paint (25H2)
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out extensive updates to Paint as part of Windows 11, enhancing its functionality while maintaining its classic appeal. This reflects the company's commitment to modernizing legacy applications.

item.title: Sam Altman’s home targeted in second attack - The San Francisco Standard
item.description: Sam Altman's home was targeted in a second attack involving a Molotov cocktail, prompting a response to a controversial article about him. This incident highlights the risks faced by prominent tech figures.

item.title: OpenAI Says Axios Breach Did Not Affect User Data
item.description: OpenAI has stated that a recent breach involving a third-party developer tool did not compromise user data. This reassurance comes amidst concerns over cybersecurity and data protection.

item.title: rappi-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The rappi-mcp package has been added to PyPI, allowing users in Chile to browse stores, search products, and manage their cart via Claude. This integration enhances the shopping experience.

item.title: rappi-mcp 0.2.1
item.description: The rappi-mcp 0.2.1 update continues to support users in Chile with features for browsing stores and managing purchases through Claude. This reflects ongoing improvements in the service.

item.title: overkill 0.5.5
item.description: Overkill 0.5.5 introduces an AI-driven code review loop that automates the review process using Codex and Claude for fixes. This enhances efficiency in software development.

item.title: synthetic-dataset-gen added to PyPI
item.description: The synthetic-dataset-gen has been added to PyPI, allowing users to generate synthetic training data for various machine learning tasks. This tool is powered by Anthropic.

item.title: Show HN: Local LLM on a Pi 4 controlling hardware via tool calling
item.description: A local LLM has been developed on a Raspberry Pi 4 that can control hardware through tool calling. This showcases the potential of lightweight AI applications.

item.title: tool-registry-py added to PyPI
item.description: The tool-registry-py has been added to PyPI, providing a typed catalogue for LLM agents to register and validate tools with zero dependencies.

item.title: claude-session-sync 0.2.5
item.description: Claude-session-sync 0.2.5 allows users to sync Claude Code sessions across different operating systems, improving accessibility and collaboration.

item.title: vcon-laptop added to PyPI
item.description: The vcon-laptop package has been introduced to PyPI, enabling the recording of laptop activity into spec-compliant vCon objects.

item.title: celesto 0.0.3
item.description: Celesto 0.0.3 is a new Python SDK and CLI for the Celesto AI platform, aimed at simplifying development with the platform's capabilities.

item.title: fast-langchain-server 0.4.1
item.description: The fast-langchain-server 0.4.1 is a production HTTP server for LangChain/LangGraph agents, offering an OpenAI-compatible API and advanced features.

item.title: Erdogan threatens to invade Israel
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan has threatened to invade Israel due to its actions in Lebanon, escalating tensions in the region. This statement reflects ongoing geopolitical conflicts.

item.title: Llimona 0.1.0
item.description: Llimona 0.1.0 is an open and modular framework for building observable LLM gateways with OpenAI-compatible APIs, facilitating integration and customization.

item.title: Apple AI Glasses Will Rival Meta’s With Several Styles, Oval Cameras - Bloomberg.com
item.description: Apple is developing AI glasses that will compete with Meta's offerings, featuring multiple styles and high-end designs. This move signals Apple's commitment to entering the augmented reality market.

item.title: End of an era at Apple as Tim Cook’s hopeful hire from Google leaves the company
item.description: Tim Cook's recent AI hire from Google is leaving Apple, marking a significant change in leadership. This departure raises questions about Apple's AI strategy moving forward.

item.title: YC Startup School: Seed-stage capital in AI not enough, large companies amassing disproportionate funding, says general partner Ankit Gupta
item.description: YC's Ankit Gupta emphasizes the need for more seed-stage capital in AI, noting that large companies are securing most funding. This reflects a growing concern about funding disparities in the startup ecosystem.

item.title: 40 Cool, Unique Tools That Come In Handy At The Perfect Time
item.description: A list of unique tools that solve specific problems, showcasing innovative solutions that can be surprisingly useful. These tools highlight the creativity in product design.

item.title: clsplusplus 7.0.0
item.description: The release of clsplusplus 7.0.0 introduces a brain-inspired memory model for large language models, allowing them to learn, recall, and forget information. This could enhance AI's cognitive capabilities.

item.title: pyisolate 0.10.2
item.description: The pyisolate 0.10.2 library enables Python developers to manage execution across multiple virtual environments, enhancing code organization and execution efficiency.

item.title: Apple’s Display-Free Smart Glasses To Debut By Early 2027, Might Sport 4 Different Designs
item.description: Apple's display-free smart glasses are set to launch by early 2027, featuring multiple designs aimed at competing with Meta's offerings. This indicates Apple's serious investment in wearable technology.

item.title: gswarp added to PyPI
item.description: The gswarp package has been added to PyPI, providing a pure-Python backend for 3D Gaussian Splatting, which could enhance graphics processing capabilities.

item.title: The Future of Everything is Lies, I Guess: Psychological Hazards
item.description: An article discussing the psychological hazards of misinformation and the implications for society. It delves into the impact of lies on trust and communication.

item.title: X slashes aggregator payouts to boost original creators
item.description: Elon Musk's X platform is reducing payouts to content aggregators to prioritize payments to original creators, aiming to foster a more authentic content ecosystem.
